narration: Start with one pointer at the left end and one at the right end.
callout: The array is sorted, so the left side is always smaller than the right side.
decision: Add the two numbers and compare the total to the target.

narration: If the sum is too small, move the left pointer right to make the total bigger.
decision: Moving the right pointer would only make the total smaller.

predictionPrompt.prompt: The current total is too small. Which pointer should move next?

choices.label: Move the left pointer right
choices.feedback: Correct. A bigger number on the left can increase the total.

choices.label: Move the right pointer left
choices.feedback: Not this time. That would make the total even smaller.

narration: Keep squeezing inward until the pair matches the target or the pointers cross.
decision: Every move throws away pairs that can no longer work.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why is it safe to move only one pointer instead of checking every pair?
reflectionPrompt.placeholder: Because the sorted order tells us...
